name: Parque Natural Lagunas Glaciares de Neila — circular from Quintanar de la Sierra
name_original: Parque Natural Lagunas Glaciares de Neila — circular desde Quintanar de la Sierra
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Quintanar De La Sierra traverse a landscape characterized by extensive Scots pine forests and picturesque lacustrine zones. The region features the Arlanza River and the River of Las Gargantas, with notable waterfalls like El Chorlón. Geological formations such as the "Calderas de Neila" and "Peña del Vaso" add to the varied terrain, offering panoramic views. The Sierra de Neila, home to a Natural Park, contributes to the area's diverse natural features. Best touring cycling routes around Quintanar De La Sierra The most popular touring cycling route is Parque Natural Lagunas Glaciares de Neila — circular from Quintanar de la Sierra, a 21.5 miles (34.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 47 minutes to complete. This route explores the glacial lagoons within the Natural Park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is San Francisco Viewpoint – Neila Lagoons loop from Quintanar de la Sierra, a difficult 18.4 miles (29.7 km) path. This route offers views from the San Francisco Viewpoint and passes by the Neila Lagoons. Local touring cyclists also love the Quintanar de la Sierra – San Leonardo de Yagüe loop from Quintanar de la Sierra, a 35.7 miles (57.4 km) trail leading through pine forests and connecting towns, often completed in about 3 hours 49 minutes. Touring cycling around Quintanar De La Sierra is defined by pine forests, lacustrine zones, and river valleys. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Quintanar De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 180 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Quintanar De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Quintanar de la Sierra?
A: There are 21 touring cycling routes available around Quintanar de la Sierra, offering a variety of experiences through the region's natural landscapes.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of the touring cycling routes in Quintanar de la Sierra?
A: The touring cycling routes in Quintanar de la Sierra are categorized as moderate and difficult. You'll find 9 moderate routes and 12 difficult routes, providing options for various skill levels.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Quintanar de la Sierra are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030253109/parque-natural-lagunas-glaciares-de-neila-circular-from-quintanar-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Lagunas Glaciares de Neila — circular from Quintanar de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option that explores the glacial lagoons.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: The terrain around Quintanar de la Sierra is characterized by extensive Scots pine forests and picturesque lacustrine zones. You'll encounter varied landscapes, including river valleys, and significant elevation changes, especially on routes leading to areas like the Sierra de Neila.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: You can explore a wealth of natural features and landmarks. Routes often pass by the Arlanza River and the River of Las Gargantas. Notable sights include the unique geological formations of the "Calderas de Neila" with their waterfalls and lakes, and the striking rock formation "Peña del Vaso" offering panoramic views. You might also encounter highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3856363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cascada Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; or various mountain peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2516913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Urbión&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites accessible by touring bike?
A: Yes, you can cycle to historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31493195" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuyacabras Necropolis loop from Quintanar de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; takes you to the Necrópolis de Cuyacabras, an archaeological site with anthropomorphic tombs carved into rock, dating back to the High Middle Ages.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Quintanar de la Sierra?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse natural landscapes, including the pine forests and lacustrine zones, and the challenging yet rewarding routes that offer stunning views and a true outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31493112" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Francisco Viewpoint – Neila Lagoons loop from Quintanar de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to offer views from the San Francisco Viewpoint. Additionally, the "Peña del Vaso" provides breathtaking panoramic views of the Sierra de la Demanda, and you can find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5585910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza Alta Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; in the region.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Quintanar de la Sierra?
A: Quintanar de la Sierra is known for its pristine environment, often called the "Costa del Oxígeno." While specific seasonal recommendations depend on personal preference, the region's natural beauty, particularly its pine forests and lacustrine zones, makes it enjoyable for cycling during milder seasons like spring and autumn when temperatures are comfortable and the scenery is vibrant.

Q: Are there any longer touring routes that connect different towns?
A: Yes, there are longer routes designed to connect towns. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31499834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintanar de la Sierra – San Leonardo de Yagüe loop from Quintanar de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is a 57.4 km trail that leads through pine forests and connects these two towns, offering a more extensive cycling experience.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the Sierra de Neila Natural Park?
A: Yes, the Sierra de Neila is a significant natural park in the area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030253109/parque-natural-lagunas-glaciares-de-neila-circular-from-quintanar-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Lagunas Glaciares de Neila — circular from Quintanar de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; route specifically explores the glacial lagoons within this Natural Park, offering stunning natural beauty.
tips: Spectacular panoramic views of the mountains, you can also see some of the Neila lagoons. Depending on the season, there may be snow on nearby peaks (even in May).

The Neila lagoons are a group of impressive lakes of glacial origin. They are located at about 1700 meters above sea level in the Sierra de la Demanda. In front of you is the Laguna Larga, with the bike you will also easily reach the Laguna Negra. There is an information panel.
The starting point for the Lagunas Glaciares de Neila natural park. The economic activity of this town is logging (sawmills, furniture factories, carpentry workshops, extraction of pine resin, etc.).
